title: League of Agents matures: the arena grows past launch - continuous-lane matches, bigger tables, published benchmarks, and a platform that runs itself

claims.text: League of Agents matures: the arena grows past launch - continuous-lane matches, bigger tables, published benchmarks, and a platform that runs itself

Continuous-lane hosting: once league-of-agents#28 lands the cmatch CLI parity verbs, a decision-point-driven, unit-scoped adapter (structurally distinct from the grid one) brings fluid speed-based matches online.
Larger tables: 3+ team matches and mixed human-agent rosters beyond the launch trio of modes (game presets exist: team-vs-bot, orchestrator-vs-bot, resident-vs-bot).
Benchmark publication cadence: versioned dataset drops mirrored to Hugging Face Datasets on a schedule, with a changelog and a citable identity per release.
Production persistence and the hosted-agent loop: platform#4 (DynamoDB/S3 wiring) and platform#5 (BYOK agents playing unattended) graduate from issues to shipped.
Live human login: platform#6 (OAuth apps + secrets) - and the first-visit walkthrough measured against the within-minutes honesty condition.
Spectator experience beyond the viewer page: replays with commentary, shareable moments, and the probe/cooperation grades surfaced as narrative.

Game-side fairness and polish follow-ups: probe discipline/rejection_rate has the same passive-rejection latent bug fixed for realization_rate in league-of-agents#33 (noted out-of-scope there); score/replay still double-read logs.

open_vagueness.text: Deploy IAM permissions (platform#7) - operator action, unblocks everything hosted
